arts.ttl: Brighton man holds three hour meeting with manager – Set to attend Bournemouth clash also
arts.sum: Brighton and Hove Albion’s Diego Gómez held a meeting with Paraguay manager Gustavo Alfaro this week that lasted for well over three hours. That’s according to Versus, who also say that Alfaro will be in attendance for Brighton’s clash with Bournemouth on Tuesday evening. arts.ttl: Analysis: Baleba and Ayari at the heart of Brighton's push for Europe
arts.sum: Hurzeler will have no hesitation in backing Baleba and Ayari to handle their latest test on Tuesday against Bournemouth, the most ferocious out-of-possession side in the Premier League. After squeezing up to press high inside the final third, Baleba even got himself a goal against Chelsea by intercepting a pass out from the back, robbing former Brighton midfielder Moises Caicedo to score, as shown below.

arts.ttl: Bournemouth aware of difficulty keeping player beyond this season – Cherries not giving up yet
arts.sum: AFC Bournemouth have enjoyed an impressive season so far under Andoni Iraola’s orders. The Premier League club are currently sixth in the table, a point behind Manchester City and Newcastle United, who are in fourth and fifth spot respectively. arts.ttl: Brighton and Southampton ‘sent emissaries’ – PL duo join Manchester United on Monday scouting mission
arts.sum: Brighton, Southampton and Manchester United all had their scouts in attendance at the Estádio do Dragão to watch FC Porto’s 1-1 draw against Vitória de Guimarães on Monday. That’s according to Tuesday’s edition of O Jogo, who state the league fixture ‘attracted a host’ of foreign clubs including Brighton, Southampton and Manchester United.
